title: Magnetic order, magnons, and crystal fields in van der Waals CeSiI
authors: Wolfgang Simeth, Connor A. Occhialini, Michael E. Ziebel, Nethmi W. Hewage, Sabrina J. Li, Daniel Pajerowski, Taehun Kim, Ben Zager, Jonathan Pelliciari, Kipton Barros, Daniel Rehn, Abhay N. Pasupathy, Valentina Bisogni, Xavier Roy, Allen Scheie
abstract: We report neutron, X-ray absorption, and resonant X-ray spectroscopy of magnetic excitations in the new heavy-fermion van-der-Waals superconductor CeSiI. We determined effective Hamiltonians and ground states of crystal electric fields and magnons. Isotropic Heisenberg interactions on a quasi two dimensional lattice, including ferromagnetic nearest-neighbor exchange as the dominant interaction, provide an excellent account to the low-energy measured dynamics and stabilize a co-rotating spin cycloid. Our study provides the basis to model CeSiI from first principles, thereby laying the ground for microscopic understanding of heavy-fermion physics, their unconventional superconductivity, and quantum criticality.
summary: This paper provides a comprehensive microscopic characterization of the magnetic properties of the van der Waals heavy-fermion superconductor CeSiI. By combining neutron and X-ray spectroscopy, the authors determine the crystal electric field levels and the Heisenberg exchange interactions that stabilize a cycloidal magnetic state. This work establishes a fundamental basis for modeling unconventional superconductivity and quantum criticality in 2D heavy-fermion systems.
main_problem: The study aims to resolve the magnetic ground state, crystal electric field (CEF) parameters, and dominant magnetic interactions in the new van der Waals heavy-fermion superconductor CeSiI.
main_result: The authors identified a co-rotating cycloidal spin spiral ground state driven by isotropic Heisenberg interactions and determined the precise CEF Hamiltonian using a multi-probe approach.
method: The research combines inelastic neutron scattering (INS), X-ray absorption spectroscopy (XAS), and resonant inelastic X-ray scattering (RIXS) with computational tools like PyCrystalField, Quanty, and Linear Spin Wave Theory.
model_or_system: The system is CeSiI, a two-dimensional van der Waals heavy-fermion material. The magnetic model utilizes an effective Heisenberg Hamiltonian with multiple exchange couplings (J1 through J12) and a CEF Hamiltonian based on Stevens operators for the Ce3+ ions.
key_observables: Magnetic excitations (magnons), CEF energy levels, X-ray linear dichroism (XLD), magnetic wave vector k, and the g-tensor.
important_parameters: Magnetic ordering temperature TN = 7.5 K, Kondo temperature TK approx 4.6 K, spin-orbit coupling zeta approx 75.69 meV, and exchange constants such as J1 = -0.93 meV.
main_assumptions: Assumes C3v symmetry for the CEF Hamiltonian and uses a point-charge model as an initial approximation for CEF parameters.
figures_summary: The figures include neutron powder spectroscopy at various energies and temperatures, comparison of different CEF models, and simulations of the magnetic spectrum and exchange interactions.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the CeSiI system, presents neutron scattering data to identify CEF modes, uses XAS/XLD to resolve CEF model degeneracy, details the magnetic exchange interactions via spin-wave theory, and concludes with the implications for heavy-fermion physics.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. (a) Trigonal crystal structure of CeSiI and conven- tional hexagonal unit cell (black skeleton, basis vectors a, b, and c). Shown are two pairs of Ce (green spheres) and the surrounding Si (gray) and I layers (purple). (b) Quasi two dimensional spin Hamiltonian that accounts for the spin dy- namics in CeSiI. Shown are 1st, 2nd, and 4th nearest neighbor bonds as introduced in the Hamiltonian (Eq. 1). (c) Magnetic ground state in agreement with our data and model. Shown are magnetic moments carried by Ce in the plane perpendic- ular to b.

FIG. 2. Crystal electric field (CEF) analysis of CeSiI, inelastic neutron scattering, X-ray absorption spectroscopy, and resonant inelastic X-ray scattering. Panels (a) and (b) show the powder neutron spectrum with Ei = 25 meV at 2 K and 60 K. Panel (c) shows the fitted model compared with the two measured CEF levels. At 60 K the crystal field levels shift to lower energies. Panel (d) shows the Q-dependence of the lowest mode intensity, which matches the Ce3+ form factor plus a small Q2 phonon background.[30] (e) Ce M5-edge XAS (881.9 eV) recorded in TEY with incident σ (blue) and π (red) polarizations at incident angle θ = 20 deg and T = 50 K. (f) XLD spectrum determined from data in (e)...

FIG. 3. Magnon dynamics in CeSiI. (a,b) Powder spectrocopy data of CeSiI taken at T = 2 K and T = 15 K, respectively. The incident neutron energy used is indicated with the vertical label on the left. In (a) we see the projected scattering of acoustic magnon branches (AB) including a pronounced Goldstone mode as well as optical branches (OB). (c) Powder spectrum calculated (calc) with the isotropic Heisenberg model in Eq. 2 (with exchange J1–J12). (d,e) Calculated spectra with anisotropic nearest neighbor exchange J1 and isotropic exchange J2–J4 (see SI for further information).
